_comment: Example machine configuration for OpenVend. Fork and modify this file for your own machine, then INSERT the rows into your Supabase tables. Selection numbering follows AMS-style conventions (rows 1-2 even numbers only, rows 3+ consecutive). Adjust selections, capacities, and product assignments to match your actual machine layout.

machine.label: Demo Office
machine.notes: 5-row combo machine. Rows 1-3 snacks, rows 4-5 beverages.

_usage_notes: 1. Pick a machine_id format you want to standardize on (MACHINE-001, OFFICE-MAIN, etc.) — see docs/09-adding-more-machines.md.
2. Set the capacity for each coil to the maximum number of units that physically fit in that slot.

3. retail_price_cents is what the machine displays. The Pi listener reads the actual sale price from the MDB frame at vend time — if those drift apart, debug your machine's pricing menu, not OpenVend.
4. Leave product_sku as null for empty slots — the dashboard will show them as 'unassigned'.

5. For initial setup, generate INSERT statements with: jq -r '.slots[] | "INSERT INTO slots (machine_id, selection, capacity, product_sku, retail_price_cents) VALUES (\u0027MACHINE-001\u0027, \u0027\(.selection)\u0027, \(.capacity), \(if .product_sku then "\u0027\(.product_sku)\u0027" else "NULL" end), \(.retail_price_cents // "NULL"));"' examples/machine-config.json
